title: Daily briefing: CRISPR gets an AI-designed upgrade
excerpt: Synthetic CRISPR proteins edit the genome more efficiently than their natural counterparts. Plus, a rocky exoplanet with an atmosphere and the mathematical wonderland of footballs. Synthetic CRISPR proteins edit the genome more efficiently than their natural counterparts. Plus, a rocky exoplanet with an atmosphere and the mathematical wonderland of footballs.
content_html:
• NATURE BRIEFING
  • 17 July 2026

Synthetic CRISPR proteins edit the genome more efficiently than their natural counterparts. Plus, a rocky exoplanet with an atmosphere and the mathematical wonderland of footballs.

By

  • Flora Graham
  1. Flora Graham

You have full access to this article via your institution.

Hello Nature readers, would you like to get this Briefing in your inbox free every day? Sign up here.

Ancient Egyptian princesses kicked ass

The remains of some ancient Egyptian princesses of the Late Middle Kingdom, who were buried with beautiful weapons, show signs that they participated in strenuous, dangerous activities such as archery and hunting. “Their bones developed to sustain heavy muscle use, which corresponds directly to the weapons discovered in their tombs,” says archeologist and study co-author Zeinab Hashesh. “These findings challenge the traditional view that elite Egyptian women led passive, sedentary lives.”

Scientific American | 7 min read

Reference: Frontiers in Environmental Archaeology paper

A rocky exoplanet with an atmosphere

Astronomers have spotted a rocky exoplanet that looks likely to have an atmosphere — and could be a host for life. The planet, LHS 1140b, appears to have a helium-rich upper atmosphere and sits in the habitable zone around a star, the area in which an orbiting planet could maintain liquid water on its surface. Researchers can’t say for sure whether there is water on the exoplanet, but the findings constitute an “amazing missing piece of the puzzle” as to whether rocky exoplanets can have atmospheres, says astrophysicist Sara Seager.

Nature | 5 min read

Reference: Science paper

CRISPR gets an AI-designed upgrade

Scientists have used artificial intelligence to design synthetic CRISPR proteins that can edit the genome more efficiently than their natural counterparts. Many of these proteins differed markedly from the originals, with some differing by roughly 30% of their amino acid sequences. Nevertheless, the synthetic versions were still able to edit genes in bacterial, plant and human cells, a finding that’s “pretty exciting” says biochemist and study co-author Jennifer Doudna, who shared the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for her work on CRISPR systems.

Nature | 5 min read

Reference: Science paper

US pushes to restrict China collaborations

Both Democrat and Republican lawmakers in the United States expressed concerns about China exploiting US research at a hearing on Wednesday. But they differed on how to protect and fund US research. The hearing follows an announcement by the US National Science Foundation, which funds about a quarter of basic US academic research, that it will ban collaborations with some international institutions, including many leading Chinese universities.

Nature | 8 min read

Features & opinion

The beautiful ball

From classic 32-panel footballs to buckyballs — the carbon molecule Buckminsterfullerene C60 — truncated icosahedrons are lots of fun. The ball being batted around in the World Cup is a curvy version nicknamed ‘Trionda’, a spherical tetrahedron. Discover more marvellous maths — including some weird and wonderful variations brought to life.

The New York Times | 11 min read

Futures: Relapse

A forgotten stack of postcards is an unwelcome temptation in the latest short story for Nature’s Futures series.

Nature | 6 min read

Five best science books this week

Andrew Robinson’s pick of the top five science books to read this week includes the science of pain and an argument against a genetic basis for wrongdoing.

Nature | 4 min read

Podcast: the ‘holy grail’ of mole-rat research

Naked mole rat (Heterocephalus glaber) queens produce a chemical that alters the production of hormones in other females, which stifles their ability to reproduce. The smell of this chemical alone is enough to maintain the strict social structure of naked mole-rat colonies, even when the queen isn’t physically present, researchers found. Identifying the specific cue that queens use to suppress reproduction in the rest of their colony is “like the holy grail for naked mole-rat research”, behavioural neuroscientist Melissa Holmes tells the Nature Podcast.

Nature Podcast | 23 min listen

Subscribe to the Nature Podcast on Apple Podcasts, Spotify or YouTube Music, or use the RSS feed.

doi: https://doi.org/10.1038/d41586-026-02272-3

This week, we search for Leif Penguinson amid the breathtaking beauty of Paratiisikuru — Paradise Gorge — in Finland. Can you find the penguin?

The answer will be in Monday’s e-mail, all thanks to Briefing photo editor and penguin wrangler Tom Houghton.

This newsletter is always evolving — tell us what you think! Please send your feedback to briefing@nature.com.

Thanks for reading,

Flora Graham, chief editor, Nature Briefing

With contributions by Jacob Smith

• Nature Briefing: AI & Robotics — 100% written by humans, of course

Synthetic CRISPR proteins edit the genome more efficiently than their natural counterparts. Plus, a rocky exoplanet with an atmosphere and the mathematical wonderland of footballs.

sentences.zh: 合成 CRISPR 蛋白比天然蛋白更有效地编辑基因组。

sentences.en: This dagger was buried alongside Ita, a daughter of the pharaoh Amenemhat II, who “was a young woman aged between 28 and 34 with strong upper-body muscle attachments, suggesting she habitually used weapons like maces or daggers”, says Hashesh.
sentences.zh: 这把匕首与法老阿蒙涅姆哈特二世的女儿伊塔 (Ita) 一起埋葬，她“是一位年龄在 28 至 34 岁之间的年轻女性，上半身肌肉发达，表明她习惯使用狼牙棒或匕首等武器”，哈什什说。

sentences.en: This dagger was buried alongside Ita, a daughter of the pharaoh Amenemhat II, who “was a young woman aged between 28 and 34 with strong upper-body muscle attachments, suggesting she habitually used weapons like maces or daggers”, says Hashesh.
sentences.zh: 这把匕首与法老阿蒙涅姆哈特二世的女儿伊塔 (Ita) 一起埋葬，她“是一位年龄在 28 至 34 岁之间的年轻女性，上半身肌肉发达，表明她习惯使用狼牙棒或匕首等武器”，哈什什说。

blocks.html_en: Ancient Egyptian princesses kicked ass

sentences.en: Ancient Egyptian princesses kicked ass
sentences.zh: 古埃及公主被踢屁股

blocks.html_en: The remains of some ancient Egyptian princesses of the Late Middle Kingdom, who were buried with beautiful weapons, show signs that they participated in strenuous, dangerous activities such as archery and hunting. “Their bones developed to sustain heavy muscle use, which corresponds directly to the weapons discovered in their tombs,” says archeologist and study co-author Zeinab Hashesh. “These findings challenge the traditional view that elite Egyptian women led passive, sedentary lives.”

sentences.en: The remains of some ancient Egyptian princesses of the Late Middle Kingdom, who were buried with beautiful weapons, show signs that they participated in strenuous, dangerous activities such as archery and hunting.
sentences.zh: 一些中王国晚期的古埃及公主的遗骸中埋藏着美丽的武器，这些遗骸表明她们参加过射箭和狩猎等艰苦、危险的活动。

sentences.en: “Their bones developed to sustain heavy muscle use, which corresponds directly to the weapons discovered in their tombs,” says archeologist and study co-author Zeinab Hashesh.
sentences.zh: 考古学家兼研究合著者泽纳布·哈希什 (Zeinab Hashesh) 表示：“他们的骨骼发育得足以维持大量肌肉的使用，这与在他们的坟墓中发现的武器直接对应。”

sentences.en: “These findings challenge the traditional view that elite Egyptian women led passive, sedentary lives.”
sentences.zh: “这些发现挑战了埃及精英女性过着被动、久坐的生活的传统观点。”

blocks.html_en: A rocky exoplanet with an atmosphere

sentences.en: A rocky exoplanet with an atmosphere
sentences.zh: 一颗有大气层的岩石系外行星

blocks.html_en: Astronomers have spotted a rocky exoplanet that looks likely to have an atmosphere — and could be a host for life. The planet, LHS 1140b, appears to have a helium-rich upper atmosphere and sits in the habitable zone around a star, the area in which an orbiting planet could maintain liquid water on its surface. Researchers can’t say for sure whether there is water on the exoplanet, but the findings constitute an “amazing missing piece of the puzzle” as to whether rocky exoplanets can have atmospheres, says astrophysicist Sara Seager.

sentences.en: Astronomers have spotted a rocky exoplanet that looks likely to have an atmosphere — and could be a host for life.
sentences.zh: 天文学家发现了一颗岩石系外行星，它看起来可能有大气层，并且可能是生命的宿主。

sentences.en: The planet, LHS 1140b, appears to have a helium-rich upper atmosphere and sits in the habitable zone around a star, the area in which an orbiting planet could maintain liquid water on its surface.
sentences.zh: 这颗行星 LHS 1140b 似乎拥有富含氦气的高层大气，位于恒星周围的宜居带中，在该区域中，绕轨道运行的行星可以在其表面维持液态水。

sentences.en: Researchers can’t say for sure whether there is water on the exoplanet, but the findings constitute an “amazing missing piece of the puzzle” as to whether rocky exoplanets can have atmospheres, says astrophysicist Sara Seager.
sentences.zh: 天体物理学家萨拉·西格表示，研究人员无法确定这颗系外行星上是否有水，但这些发现构成了关于岩石系外行星是否有大气层的“令人惊讶的缺失部分”。

blocks.html_en: CRISPR gets an AI-designed upgrade

sentences.en: CRISPR gets an AI-designed upgrade
sentences.zh: CRISPR 获得人工智能设计的升级

blocks.html_en: Scientists have used artificial intelligence to design synthetic CRISPR proteins that can edit the genome more efficiently than their natural counterparts. Many of these proteins differed markedly from the originals, with some differing by roughly 30% of their amino acid sequences. Nevertheless, the synthetic versions were still able to edit genes in bacterial, plant and human cells, a finding that’s “pretty exciting” says biochemist and study co-author Jennifer Doudna, who shared the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for her work on CRISPR systems.

sentences.en: Scientists have used artificial intelligence to design synthetic CRISPR proteins that can edit the genome more efficiently than their natural counterparts.
sentences.zh: 科学家们利用人工智能设计了合成的 CRISPR 蛋白，与天然蛋白相比，它可以更有效地编辑基因组。

sentences.en: Many of these proteins differed markedly from the originals, with some differing by roughly 30% of their amino acid sequences.
sentences.zh: 其中许多蛋白质与原始蛋白质显着不同，其中一些蛋白质的氨基酸序列相差约 30%。

sentences.en: Nevertheless, the synthetic versions were still able to edit genes in bacterial, plant and human cells, a finding that’s “pretty exciting” says biochemist and study co-author Jennifer Doudna, who shared the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for her work on CRISPR systems.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，合成版本仍然能够编辑细菌、植物和人类细胞中的基因，这一发现“非常令人兴奋”，生物化学家兼研究合著者 Jennifer Doudna 表示，她因在 CRISPR 系统方面的工作而获得了 2020 年诺贝尔化学奖。

blocks.html_en: Both Democrat and Republican lawmakers in the United States expressed concerns about China exploiting US research at a hearing on Wednesday. But they differed on how to protect and fund US research. The hearing follows an announcement by the US National Science Foundation, which funds about a quarter of basic US academic research, that it will ban collaborations with some international institutions, including many leading Chinese universities.

sentences.en: Both Democrat and Republican lawmakers in the United States expressed concerns about China exploiting US research at a hearing on Wednesday.
sentences.zh: 美国民主党和共和党议员在周三的听证会上都表达了对中国利用美国研究的担忧。

sentences.en: But they differed on how to protect and fund US research.
sentences.zh: 但他们在如何保护和资助美国研究方面存在分歧。

sentences.en: The hearing follows an announcement by the US National Science Foundation, which funds about a quarter of basic US academic research, that it will ban collaborations with some international institutions, including many leading Chinese universities.
sentences.zh: 听证会之前，资助美国约四分之一基础学术研究的美国国家科学基金会宣布，将禁止与一些国际机构（包括许多中国领先大学）合作。

sentences.en: Podcast: the ‘holy grail’ of mole-rat research
sentences.zh: 播客：鼹鼠研究的“圣杯”

blocks.html_en: Naked mole rat (Heterocephalus glaber) queens produce a chemical that alters the production of hormones in other females, which stifles their ability to reproduce. The smell of this chemical alone is enough to maintain the strict social structure of naked mole-rat colonies, even when the queen isn’t physically present, researchers found. Identifying the specific cue that queens use to suppress reproduction in the rest of their colony is “like the holy grail for naked mole-rat research”, behavioural neuroscientist Melissa Holmes tells the Nature Podcast.

sentences.en: Naked mole rat (Heterocephalus glaber) queens produce a chemical that alters the production of hormones in other females, which stifles their ability to reproduce.
sentences.zh: 裸鼹鼠（Heterocephalus glaber）女王会产生一种化学物质，这种化学物质会改变其他雌性体内激素的产生，从而抑制它们的繁殖能力。

sentences.en: The smell of this chemical alone is enough to maintain the strict social structure of naked mole-rat colonies, even when the queen isn’t physically present, researchers found.
sentences.zh: 研究人员发现，即使女王不在场，仅这种化学物质的气味就足以维持裸鼹鼠群体严格的社会结构。

sentences.en: Identifying the specific cue that queens use to suppress reproduction in the rest of their colony is “like the holy grail for naked mole-rat research”, behavioural neuroscientist Melissa Holmes tells the Nature Podcast.
sentences.zh: 行为神经科学家梅丽莎·霍姆斯（Melissa Holmes）告诉《自然》播客，识别出蜂王用来抑制群体其他成员繁殖的特定线索“就像裸鼹鼠研究的圣杯”。

blocks.html_en: This week, we search for Leif Penguinson amid the breathtaking beauty of Paratiisikuru — Paradise Gorge — in Finland. Can you find the penguin?

sentences.en: This week, we search for Leif Penguinson amid the breathtaking beauty of Paratiisikuru — Paradise Gorge — in Finland.
sentences.zh: 本周，我们在芬兰帕拉蒂斯库鲁（天堂峡谷）令人惊叹的美景中寻找莱夫企鹅。

sentences.en: Can you find the penguin?

content_html_zh:
• 自然简报
  • 17 July 2026

合成 CRISPR 蛋白比天然蛋白更有效地编辑基因组。 另外，还有一颗岩石系外行星，拥有大气层和足球的数学仙境。

By

  • 弗洛拉·格雷厄姆
  1. 弗洛拉·格雷厄姆

您可以通过您的机构完全访问本文。

《自然》杂志的读者们大家好，您想每天免费在收件箱中收到这份简报吗？ 在这里注册。

古埃及公主被踢屁股

一些中王国晚期的古埃及公主的遗骸中埋藏着美丽的武器，这些遗骸表明她们参加过射箭和狩猎等艰苦、危险的活动。 考古学家兼研究合著者泽纳布·哈希什 (Zeinab Hashesh) 表示：“他们的骨骼发育得足以维持大量肌肉的使用，这与在他们的坟墓中发现的武器直接对应。” “这些发现挑战了埃及精英女性过着被动、久坐的生活的传统观点。”

科学美国人 | 7 分钟阅读

参考文献：环境考古学前沿论文

一颗有大气层的岩石系外行星

天文学家发现了一颗岩石系外行星，它看起来可能有大气层，并且可能是生命的宿主。 这颗行星 LHS 1140b 似乎拥有富含氦气的高层大气，位于恒星周围的宜居带中，在该区域中，绕轨道运行的行星可以在其表面维持液态水。 天体物理学家萨拉·西格表示，研究人员无法确定这颗系外行星上是否有水，但这些发现构成了关于岩石系外行星是否有大气层的“令人惊讶的缺失部分”。

自然 | 5 分钟阅读

参考文献：科学论文

CRISPR 获得人工智能设计的升级

科学家们利用人工智能设计了合成的 CRISPR 蛋白，与天然蛋白相比，它可以更有效地编辑基因组。 其中许多蛋白质与原始蛋白质显着不同，其中一些蛋白质的氨基酸序列相差约 30%。 尽管如此，合成版本仍然能够编辑细菌、植物和人类细胞中的基因，这一发现“非常令人兴奋”，生物化学家兼研究合著者 Jennifer Doudna 表示，她因在 CRISPR 系统方面的工作而获得了 2020 年诺贝尔化学奖。

自然 | 5 分钟阅读

参考文献：科学论文

美国推动限制中国合作

美国民主党和共和党议员在周三的听证会上都表达了对中国利用美国研究的担忧。 但他们在如何保护和资助美国研究方面存在分歧。 听证会之前，资助美国约四分之一基础学术研究的美国国家科学基金会宣布，将禁止与一些国际机构（包括许多中国领先大学）合作。

自然 | 8 分钟阅读

特点及意见

美丽的球

从经典的 32 块足球到巴基球（碳分子巴克敏斯特富勒烯 C60），截断的二十面体非常有趣。 世界杯上使用的球是一种弯曲的球，绰号“Trionda”，是一种球形四面体。 发现更多奇妙的数学——包括一些栩栩如生的奇怪而奇妙的变化。

纽约时报 |阅读时间 11 分钟

期货：复发

在《自然的未来》系列的最新短篇小说中，一叠被遗忘的明信片是一种不受欢迎的诱惑。

自然 | 6 分钟阅读

本周五本最佳科学书籍

安德鲁·罗宾逊（Andrew Robinson）选出了本周最值得阅读的五本科学书籍，其中包括疼痛科学和反对不当行为的遗传基础的论点。

自然 | 4 分钟阅读

播客：鼹鼠研究的“圣杯”

裸鼹鼠（Heterocephalus glaber）女王会产生一种化学物质，这种化学物质会改变其他雌性体内激素的产生，从而抑制它们的繁殖能力。 研究人员发现，即使女王不在场，仅这种化学物质的气味就足以维持裸鼹鼠群体严格的社会结构。 行为神经科学家梅丽莎·霍姆斯（Melissa Holmes）告诉《自然》播客，识别出蜂王用来抑制群体其他成员繁殖的特定线索“就像裸鼹鼠研究的圣杯”。

自然播客 | 23分钟听

在 Apple Podcasts、Spotify 或 YouTube Music 上订阅 Nature Podcast，或使用 RSS feed。

doi：https://doi.org/10.1038/d41586-026-02272-3

本周，我们在芬兰帕拉蒂斯库鲁（天堂峡谷）令人惊叹的美景中寻找莱夫企鹅。 你能找到企鹅吗？

答案将在周一的电子邮件中，这一切都要归功于简报照片编辑和企鹅管理员汤姆霍顿。

本时事通讯始终在不断发展——请告诉我们您的想法！ 请将您的反馈发送至briefing@nature.com。

感谢您的阅读，

弗洛拉·格雷厄姆，《自然简报》主编
